preamble: The transition from a glottal stop to the vowel [u] is a form of vocalization that often involves an intermediate labialized stage [ʔʷ], commonly found in languages with complex laryngeal systems.

examples.note: In some Salishan languages, labialized glottal stops vocalize to [u] in certain morphological or phonotactic contexts. The labial feature is retained as vowel roundedness.

sources: Kinkade, M. D. (1991). Salishan languages. In W. Bright (ed.), International Encyclopedia of Linguistics. Oxford University Press.
